input.title: Tesla Autopilot — Wrongful death case (representative post-Banner consolidated docket)
input.description: Plaintiffs allege Tesla Autopilot system failed to detect stationary obstacle / crossing pedestrian / lane geometry and caused fatal collision while driver had system engaged in conditions outside Operational Design Domain. Pattern across cases: Tesla's marketing of 'Full Self-Driving' alleged to induce over-reliance; sensor suite (camera-only post-radar-removal) alleged insufficient for ODD claimed.

events.description: Tesla markets 'Full Self-Driving' capability while EULA disclaims same; consumer-perception evidence shows induced over-reliance
Driver engages Autopilot in conditions arguably outside published Operational Design Domain (highway-only stated, used on surface street with intersection)
Camera-only sensor stack fails to detect stationary obstacle at intersection — class of failure documented in NHTSA investigations of vision-only stack post-radar-removal
Autopilot neural net fails to classify obstacle; no emergency-braking activation in available pre-impact window
Driver hands-on-wheel monitoring obligation not met; documented attention lapse contemporaneous with crash
Collision occurs; fatal injury to other party

prediction._note: Anchor-log prediction. Produced by the same deterministic scoring engine that runs at faultkey.com/try. Sealed by SHA256 commitment + OpenTimestamps + Sigstore Rekor; revealed when the underlying case resolves.
